title: The CTO’s Context Dilemma
author: Kapil Viren Ahuja
text:
Member-only story
The CTO’s Context Dilemma
Here’s the 7-dimension framework for the decision most teams are getting wrong - plus a ready-to-use scoring template and 3 AI prompts to evaluate your own setup.
The Grep That Broke the Pipeline
I had been wrong for five months. I just hadn’t told anyone yet.
Tanu walked into my office with the kind of urgency that meant either the build was broken, or someone had challenged her architecture. Today, she carried her laptop like a weapon.
“You need to see this,” she said, setting it down and pulling up the RAG pipeline she had spent three weeks building for our coding agents. Vector database, embedding pipeline, reranker, orchestration layer — the works. Six components, carefully tuned, running on infrastructure that cost us $380 to $3,300 a month before we processed a single query.
I turned my laptop around to face her. On my screen: Claude Code navigating the PhoenixOS codebase. Grep. Glob. Read. A 50-year-old Unix utility and two of its friends.
Here is the part I should have told her sooner.
